blocked_reason: The safe c=1 selected-MoE post-op fold is already the default path; the next direct down+combine fusion would collapse selected-expert grid parallelism and has prior parent evidence of a slowdown.

already_fused_work: selected-expert weighted sum over num_experts_per_tok rows
current_path_audit.dispatch_floor_remaining: one combine launch per MoE layer remains (40 calls/token in M.4) because fusing into selected down GEMV would need either atomics or reduced parallelism.
current_path_audit.grouped_prefill_note: The sorted-lane grouped prefill path still uses weighted_lanes_sum_out + shared_gate_combine_residual_batch, but that path is tokens>1 prefill only and cannot improve 512/128 decode.

micro_probe.conclusion: No runtime thread-count lever; the fused c=1 combine kernel is launch-floor-sized at ~2.44 us for rows=8, features=2048.

parent_negative_evidence.prototype: selected down-projection + weighted expert sum + shared-gate combine + residual add
parent_negative_evidence.correctness: bit-for-bit on rows=8, in=512, out=2048, experts=256, FP16
parent_negative_evidence.reason: The fused kernel removed one launch and an intermediate but collapsed grid parallelism from out_pack * active_experts blocks to out_pack blocks; graph replay already amortizes the launch saving.

benchmarks.interpretation: Run-to-run noise/current baseline only; no D1.4 candidate was defaulted and no 512/128 decode improvement is claimed.

decision.stop_condition: The dataflow-permitted c=1 post-op fold is already active and correct. The next direct fusion that would remove the remaining combine launch is rejected by parent target-shape evidence because it abandons selected-expert parallelism; thread probing found no smaller combine-kernel lever.
decision.future_reopen_condition: Only revisit if a design preserves per-expert/out-pack parallelism while avoiding atomics, or if a graph-level fusion can remove the dispatch without changing the selected down GEMV grid.
